# PySeascape Library

{{ pyansys }} {{ python }} {{ pypi }} {{ GH-CI }} {{ build }} {{ MIT }} {{ black }}

A pythonic remotable interface to RedHawkSC and TotemSC that allows integration with other PyAnsys and Python libraries.

## How to install

### Install from PyPI

User installation can be performed by running:

```bash
pip install ansys-seascape
```

OR

```bash
python -m pip install ansys-seascape
```

### Install from latest Github source

Fetch latest source from github:

```bash
cd <your-library-directory>
git clone https://github.com/pyansys/pyseascape.git
```

(Optional) Create and enable virtual environment. Please refer to official [venv] documentation for more help regarding virtual environment setup.

```bash
# Create a virtual environment
python -m venv .venv

# Activate it in a POSIX system
source .venv/bin/activate

# Activate it in Windows CMD environment
.venv\Scripts\activate.bat

# Activate it in Windows Powershell
.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
```

Install the project

```bash
cd pyseascape
pip install .
```

Install additional requirements (if needed):

```bash
python -m pip install .[tests]
python -m pip install .[doc]
```

## Usage

*Note: Either a local installation or remote connection to licensed RedHawk-SC is required to use the pyseascape library. This only offers a remotable frontend interface that can run directly in native Python on any machine.*

### Launching local RedHawk-SC in backend

```python
from ansys.seascape import RedHawkSC
gp = RedHawkSC(executable=path_to_executable)
```

OR

```python
from ansys import seascape
gp = seascape.RedHawkSC(executable=path_to_executable)
```

### Connecting to remote RedHawk-SC session

```python
from ansys.seascape import RedHawkSC
gp = RedHawkSC(url=url_or_ip_to_redhawksc_server:port)
```

All RedHawk-SC global functions can be called using prefix of RedHawkSC object name. Object methods can be called as normal.

### Running RedHawk-SC commands

For example:

```python
# If gp = RedHawkSC(...)
db = gp.open_db(db_name)  # Returns a SeascapeDB remotable object
db.create_design_view(...)

# Creating RedHawk-SC objects
inst = gp.Instance('Inst_Name')

# RedHawk-SC modules must also be prefixed by gp
# E.g. using voltage_impact module
gp.voltage_impact.helpers.get_pgimpact_histograms(...)
```

## Accessing RedHawk-SC help

RedHawk-SC native help function supports command based as well as keyword based help.
This help can be accessed remotely as well.

```python
# If gp = RedHawkSC(...)

# command based help
gp.help(command='gp.Scatter')

# keyword based help
gp.help(keyword='scatter')
```

## Known issues and limitations

GUI features have not yet been implemented. Hence, commands like open_console_window, open_scheduler_window etc. do not work yet. Commands like gp.scatter_plot also does not work as it requires drawing gui plots

## Documentation

Please refer to RedHawk-SC Documentation.

functions.name: ansys.seascape.utils.include
documentation:
Include and evaluate given script

Parameters
----------
file_name: str
    File name of the script to evaluate

functions.name: ansys.seascape.utils.write_to_file
documentation:
Write given args and kwargs into specified file.

Parameters
----------
file_name: str
    Name of the file to be written
args: iterable
    zero or more parameters that will be written to file, each on new line.
kwargs: dict
    zero or more `key = value` pairs that will be written to file, each on new line.

variables.name: ansys.seascape.utils._include_path_stack
documentation: run-dir is always includable relatively

classes.name: ansys.seascape.redhawk.RedHawkSC
documentation: A proxy class to handle the connection to RedHawk-SC installation.
RedHawkSC is same as SCApp for now but can be specialized later

functions.name: ansys.seascape.scapp._inject_global_commands
documentation:
This is a utility function to define commonly used symbols in existing scripts
For example, without this, it is mandatory to write gp.open_db instead of just open_db

classes.name: ansys.seascape.scapp.Instance
documentation: Provides remotable proxy to Instance class in RedHawk-SC.

classes.name: ansys.seascape.scapp.Pin
documentation: Provides remotable proxy to Pin class in RedHawk-SC.

classes.name: ansys.seascape.scapp.Net
documentation: Provides remotable proxy to Net class in RedHawk-SC.

classes.name: ansys.seascape.scapp.Layer
documentation: Provides remotable proxy to Layer class in RedHawk-SC.

classes.name: ansys.seascape.scapp.Remotable
documentation: Remotable classes are those that have remotable APIs on the server side

classes.name: ansys.seascape.scapp.View
documentation: Provides remotable proxy to all View type classes in RedHawk-SC.

classes.name: ansys.seascape.scapp.SeaScapeDB
documentation: Provides remotable proxy to SeascapeDB class in RedHawk-SC.

classes.name: ansys.seascape.scapp.LayoutWindow
documentation: Provides handler to LayoutWindow in RedHawk-SC.

classes.name: ansys.seascape.scapp.Launcher
documentation: Provides remotable proxy to job launcher in RedHawk-SC.

methods.name: ansys.seascape.scapp.XTExtractTweaks.__init__
documentation: defined as a hack to get around the extractor options problem

classes.name: ansys.seascape.scapp.OptionsBundle
documentation: Provides pythonic representation to options passed to most views during their creation.

classes.name: ansys.seascape.scapp.SCAppModule
documentation: Supports util function calls that are passed into view creation.

classes.name: ansys.seascape.scapp.ObjConverter
documentation:
hierarchical json converter to objects like Instance, Pin, ...
Works for dict, list, ... recursively
